summary: A publisher's description of Daphne Koller's textbook, Probabilistic Graphical Models. It outlines her approach to reasoning under uncertainty using model-based, probabilistic frameworks that rely on representation, inference, and learning.

principles.statement: Models must make uncertainty explicit to be faithful to reality.
principles.rationale: Because uncertainty is an inescapable aspect of most real-world applications, automated systems need probabilistic models to accurately reason and make decisions.
principles.source_quote: Because uncertainty is an inescapable aspect of most real-world applications, the book focuses on probabilistic models, which make the uncertainty explicit and provide models that are more faithful to reality.

frameworks.name: Probabilistic Graphical Models (PGMs)
frameworks.when_to_apply: When building automated systems that need to reason, reach conclusions, or make decisions based on complex, uncertain information.
steps: Representation: Construct an interpretable model of the complex system.
Inference: Manipulate the model using reasoning algorithms to reach conclusions.
Learning: Automatically learn or refine the models from data when manual construction is difficult.
frameworks.source_quote: For each class of models, the text describes the three fundamental cornerstones: representation, inference, and learning, presenting both basic concepts and advanced techniques.

mental_models.name: Model-based reasoning
mental_models.description: An approach to artificial intelligence where interpretable models of complex systems are constructed (either manually or learned from data) and then manipulated by reasoning algorithms to reach conclusions.
